meaning: n.[机]嵌齿,小船vt.上齿轮,欺骗
examples: In general, our society is becoming one of the giant enterprises directed by a bureaucratic management in which man becomes a small well-oiled cog in the machinery.
Mr. Lake was an important cog in the republican campaign machine.
Oil stocks of interest are APC, COG, KOG, MRO, XOM, CVX, MUR, and DVN.
Larger more traditional companies treat GCs as more of a cog such as the controller or product manager.
